title: Adhesive tape dispenser with tape alignment mechanism
abstract: An adhesive tape dispensing device with a tape alignment mechanism, comprising a body formed from a rigid material or materials, configured to hold and dispense adhesive tape, and a tape alignment mechanism comprising at least one spring that presses laterally against the edge of the tape, the side of the tape form, or against a hub onto which the tape is mounted, wherein one edge of the portion of tape being dispensed is kept in a fixed alignment relative to the dispensing device while it is being dispensed.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An adhesive tape dispensing device with a tape alignment mechanism, comprising: a body formed from a rigid material or materials, configured to hold and dispense adhesive tape; and a tape alignment mechanism comprising at least one spring that presses laterally against the edge of the tape, the side of the tape form, or against a hub onto which ihe tape is mounted, wherein one edge of the portion of tape being dispensed is kept in a fixed alignment relative to the dispensing device while it is being dispensed. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the at least one spring is a leaf spring. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the at least one leaf spring is molded or formed from the body of the device, 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the at least one leaf spring further comprises a nodular tip, 5. An adhesive tape dispensing device with a tape alignment mechanism, comprising: a body formed from a rigid material or materials, configured to hold and dispense adhesive tape; and a tape alignment mechanism comprising a holder with a tapered shape onto which a roll of tape is placed, wherein, while the tape is being dispensed, and the roll of tape is held in a fixed alignment relative to the dispensing device due to a force pressing the inside edge of the roll of tape down against the holder with a tapered shape tending to push the roll of tape toward the narrow end of the holder with a tapered shape, 6. The device of claim 5, wherein the wide end and narrow end of the holder with a tapered shape are in a horizontal position relative to the ground while tape is being dispensed, and the force is the force of gravity. 7. The device of claim 6, further comprising a spring that pushes onto the surface of the roll of tape from the outside toward the holder with a tapered shape, wherein the force is the force generated by the spring. 8. The device of claim 7, wherein, while tape is being dispensed, the force of the spring is oriented in the same direction as the force of gravity, and the combination of both forces holds the tape roll in alignment. 9. A stripwise adhesive dispensing device, comprising: a bisected ladhrymiform body formed from a rigid material and having a bottom portion having a first width, a tapered top portion having a smaller second width, a plurality of circular cut-outs formed into the body; a cutting blade; and a plurality (or singularity', as in a single wave spring of springs; wherein at least a circular cut-out is configured to hold a spring; wherein the bisected lachiymiform body and the held spring are configured to hold a roll of stripwise adhesive securely in place; and wherein the held stepwise adhesive is positioned and oriented for dispensation without interfering with or damaging an adhesive portion of the held stripwise adhesive. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the bottom portion further comprises: a plurality of cut-outs; a plurality of stationary lever arms, each lever arm further comprising at least a nodular tip; and wherein the plurality' of lever arms are configured to hold stripwise adhesive. I f. The device of claim 10, wherein the stripwise adhesive is configured as a roll. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the top portion further comprises an additional cut out, wherein the cutting blade is affixed to the additional cut-out. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the cuting blade is configured to be movable to accommodate width variation of the held stripwise adhesive.
